name: Sildenafil hypoxia review supports only adjacent and unreliable PDE5 exercise-performance transfer
statement: A systematic review of sildenafil in healthy humans under hypoxia found pulmonary hemodynamic and oxygenation effects but concluded that sildenafil had no reliable effect on exercise performance; any relevance to tadalafil is adjacent class evidence only.
population_or_context: Healthy humans in hypoxia studies using sildenafil.
time_horizon: Acute hypoxia exercise studies through August 2018.
quantitative_note: Fourteen studies included 210 subjects receiving sildenafil 40, 50, or 100 mg/day; effects were large for lowering pulmonary artery pressure, moderate/small for oxygenation and cardiac output, and no reliable performance effect overall.

source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: Abstract reports systematic review eligibility, included trials, effect directions, subgroup notes, and conclusion.

caveats: Performance effects varied by altitude model.
population_profile: Healthy mixed adults under hypoxia; low directness to tadalafil in normoxic healthy men.
tadalafil_exposure: No tadalafil; sildenafil 40, 50, or 100 mg/day in included studies.
endpoint_measure: Pulmonary artery pressure, cardiac output, SpO2, exercise performance under hypoxia.
effect_estimate: No reliable performance improvement overall; possible larger SpO2/performance signal at terrestrial hypobaric altitude than normobaric hypoxia.
safety_findings: Sildenafil safety not extracted in detail; class safety cannot be assumed identical to tadalafil due exposure differences.
directness_to_target_population: Low to moderate for healthy hypoxia physiology, low for tadalafil and normoxic gym performance.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Agent mismatch, heterogeneous altitude protocols, small trials, and performance-effect variability.
class_transfer_limits: Adjacent sildenafil evidence cannot establish tadalafil benefit; tadalafil-specific altitude/performance trials would be needed.
